input.background: We wish to estimate the mean weight of a population in which body weight has a log-Normal distribution with $\sigma=40$ pounds. Three independent SRS each of $n=712$ are selected from the population.
input.question: Calculate $95 \%$ confidence intervals for $\mu$ based on the first sample with $\bar{x}_1=183$.

output.answer: For sample 1, the confidence interval is $\bar{x}_1 \pm m=183 \pm 3=180$ to 186 (pounds).

sigma.description: The standard deviation of the log-Normal distribution of body weight in the population.

n.description: The sample size of each simple random sample (SRS) selected from the population.

x_bar_1.description: The sample mean of the first simple random sample (SRS).

confidence_level.description: The confidence level for constructing the confidence interval for the population mean.

meta_info.caption: 95 % confidence intervals for $\mu, \sigma$ known (Body weight, 20-29-year-old males).
meta_info.section: 10.2 Confidence Intervals for $\\mu$ When $\\sigma$ Is Known
